subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Acquire and preprocess open-source models and evaluation datasets, complete the software and hardware environment configuration required for the proposed solution

subsections.title: Participants
content: 1. Proficient in various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with frontier and popular open/closed-source large model resources, with personal usage experience, summaries, and insights

2. Proficient in NLP classic deep learning models (such as Transformer series, LLaMA series, GLM series, etc.) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs, etc.); master at least one common deep learning development framework, such as PyTorch; maintain continuous attention and interest in the working principles and latest news of large-scale generative language models (LLMs) after GPT-3.5

3. Proficient in Python, able to use basic regular expressions and command-line scripts; familiar with NLP basic concepts and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation, etc.); able to proficiently use common NLP open-source libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba, etc.)
4. Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-motivation

subsections.title: Input
content: - Input Name: Resources required for environment configuration

Check whether the hardware of the running environment meets the following requirements: GPU preferably NVIDIA A10 or above, GPU with VRAM ≥ 16GB, CPU ≥ 8 cores, RAM ≥ 32GB, operating system Linux (Ubuntu 20.04+).
Download and install corresponding software resources:
Download LLM XiYanSQL-QwenCoder related code, understand its basic usage
(Optional) Download and read BIRD evaluation dataset literature, understand its basic information and usage
Please list a checklist for self-inspection:
4. BIRD Evaluation Dataset (including training and validation sets) Github: https://bird-bench.github.io/
5. BIRD Evaluation Dataset Literature arXiv: https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.04701

subsections.title: Output
content: - Output Name: Environment configuration resources ready

- Output Description: Server configured with GPU driver, Docker, vLLM, meeting hardware and system requirements for model deployment; BIRD Bench dataset downloaded and extracted. Related environment shows "configuration successful", dataset downloaded locally displayed as files.

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: After the environment configuration from previous step is ready, this step completes the model file download and configuration for the LLM required by the solution, and verifies whether the model can run normally and implement NL2SQL functionality.

subsections.title: Participants
content: 1. Proficient in various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with frontier and popular open/closed-source large model resources, with personal usage experience, summaries, and insights

2. Proficient in NLP classic deep learning models (such as Transformer series, LLaMA series, GLM series, etc.) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs, etc.); master at least one common deep learning development framework, such as PyTorch; maintain continuous attention and interest in the working principles and latest news of large-scale generative language models (LLMs) after GPT-3.5

3. Proficient in Python, able to use basic regular expressions and command-line scripts; familiar with NLP basic concepts and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation, etc.); able to proficiently use common NLP open-source libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba, etc.)
4. Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-motivation

subsections.title: Input
content: - Input Name: Ready environment, model configuration resources
Install and configure downloaded model files
Run LLM and confirm normal operation
- Input Example: Model download and model deployment
model_path = "XGenerationLab/XiYanSQL-QwenCoder-32B-2504" # Replace with local model path
llm = LLM(model=model_path, tensor_parallel_size=8) # Adjust tensor_parallel_size according to GPU count

subsections.title: Output
content: - Output Description: Model installed and running successfully, able to receive natural language question input and accurately output corresponding SQL statements.

steps.title: Evaluation Based on Bird Bench Dataset

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Use Bird Bench dev (validation set) to evaluate the deployed model, calculate core metrics, compare with leaderboard baseline performance, ensure the model meets current solution requirements on a runnable basis.

subsections.title: Participants
content: 1. Proficient in various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with frontier and popular open/closed-source large model resources, with personal usage experience, summaries, and insights

2. Proficient in NLP classic deep learning models (such as Transformer series, LLaMA series, GLM series, etc.) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs, etc.); master at least one common deep learning development framework, such as PyTorch; maintain continuous attention and interest in the working principles and latest news of large-scale generative language models (LLMs) after GPT-3.5

3. Proficient in Python, able to use basic regular expressions and command-line scripts; familiar with NLP basic concepts and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation, etc.); able to proficiently use common NLP open-source libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba, etc.)
4. Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-motivation

subsections.title: Input
content: - Input Name: Ready model, ready Bird Bench validation set, evaluation script
Ensure model and validation set are ready
Obtain Bird Bench evaluation script, ensure script can run normally
Run model on validation set, use evaluation script for metric evaluation, record evaluation results, compare with Bird Bench official published baseline
Run evaluation script in command-line format:
1. Bird bench evaluation script GitHub: https://github.com/arcwisedata/bird-sql

subsections.title: Output
content: - Output Description: Output model results on evaluation dataset, including logical accuracy, execution accuracy and other metrics. After comparing with Bird Bench official published baseline, results are labeled, meeting solution requirements
